summary: Both round-1 majors are cleanly closed and the five minors are substantively addressed. The blank-rename litmus now pins the exact regenerated title ("Привет" — correct for AutoTitleGenerator's ≤MAX_LEN(20) branch, 6 chars Cyrillic) and an added companion test pins the fallback branch ("Чат от " prefix, no user-message seed). Date-boundary coverage is pinned both on the day-count axis ({0,1,2,6,7,10} day offsets exercise the 2..6 inclusive range and the day-7 off-by-one) and on the wall-clock axis (sectionBoundaryIsLocalMidnightNotRolling24Hours lands a 23:59-yesterday timestamp into YESTERDAY, which a naive 24h-delta rewrite would route to TODAY). checkModelAvailableReturnsFalseWhenModelRemovedFromRegistry cleanly exercises the second branch of the new impl (chat exists, registry empty) and is incremental over WhenChatUnknown (which exercises the first branch: chatDao.getById returning null). The 60-char cap test pins length==60, which is the proper failure signal for any off-by-one in .take(MAX_MANUAL_TITLE_LEN). 18 tests, 0 litmus failures; one low-value tightening noted below but not blocking.

findings.issue: The assertion checks only `call.title.length == 60`, not the content. A broken impl that wrote `"B".repeat(60)` or some other 60-char constant would still pass. This is an unlikely failure mode for a `.take(N)` bug (wrong N yields wrong length), but the current assertion also does not pin the trim-before-take ordering — e.g. `"   " + "A".repeat(70)` would, under `.trim().take(60)`, return 60 A's, whereas a bug that did `.take(60).trim()` would return 57 A's. The length-only assertion is therefore one-sided: it catches most off-by-one bugs on the cap, but is blind to ordering regressions.
findings.recommendation: Tighten the content assertion: `assertEquals("A".repeat(60), call.title)`. Optionally add a second case with leading whitespace to pin the trim-before-take ordering: `viewModel.renameChat(chatId = 81L, newTitle = "   " + "A".repeat(70))` followed by `assertEquals("A".repeat(60), chatRepo.titleUpdates.last().title)` — proves trim runs before take (a reversed order would yield 57 A's).

blankRenameLitmus.note: assertEquals("Привет", call.title) pins AutoTitleGenerator output for the ≤MAX_LEN(20) branch. A broken impl that wrote back chat.title ("Old manual") would fail. Companion test pins the fallback branch via startsWith("Чат от ").

dateBoundaries.note: dateBucketsHonorExactDayBoundaries exercises {0,1,2,6,7,10} offsets — catches any edit to the `2L..6L` range. Tightening it to `1L..6L`, `2L..7L`, or `3L..6L` all produce distinct section assignments that the test asserts on. sectionBoundaryIsLocalMidnightNotRolling24Hours lands 23:59-yesterday into YESTERDAY: a rewrite that computed `(today_midnight_ms - lastMessageAt) / DAY_MS` would return 0 (1 minute delta) and misroute to TODAY, failing this test.

sixtyCharCap.note: length == 60 assertion catches any wrong-N bug on .take(). Content fidelity and trim-before-take ordering are not asserted — see minor finding above.

modelRemovedFromRegistry.note: The new test is incremental: it exercises the second branch of the reworked impl (chatDao.getById returns a chat, registry.models.value.firstOrNull returns null → false), whereas WhenChatUnknown exercises the first branch (chatDao.getById returns null → false). Removing either branch from the impl would fail exactly one of the two tests.

localMidnightNot24Hour.note: Decision 7 claims local-date granularity. The test pins that claim: classifyDate receives date=Instant.ofEpochMilli(23:59-yesterday).atZone.toLocalDate() == today.minusDays(1), so ChronoUnit.DAYS.between → 1 → YESTERDAY. A naive 24h-delta impl using millisecond subtraction would compute ~1-minute delta and classify as TODAY — the test's assertEquals(listOf(200L), yesterday.chats.map { it.id }) would fail because YESTERDAY section would be empty/missing.
